claim: Under the user's variational +sqrt(N)_xx/(2 sqrt(N)) sign, does the Mcs surface m = u - N*phi_x = 0 act as a dynamical attractor of B-NLS starting from u(x,0) = N*phi_x + eps*cos(2 pi x / L)? Characterize ||m||_2(t) over T=12: decay law (exp, power, oscillatory), timescale tau or alpha, eps-dependence.

method: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step with USER's sign convention. Psi=sqrt(N)exp(i phi_tilde) where phi=0.5x+phi_tilde. Kinetic propagator uses exp(+i k^2 dt/2) (flipped from standard NLS to absorb +Q). u integrated as independent real field via spectral RK4 on m_t + (u m)_x + m u_x = 0 reformulated as u_t = u_x*(N*phi_x - u) - N*(phi_x)_t. Strang split on Psi + RK4 on u. 2/3 dealiasing.
bank_use_rationale: kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure rules out direct (N,phi) RK4 as a meaningful baseline (soliton has min(N)~1e-7 noise floor). kb-nls-madelung-psi-handles-zero-density supports Psi representation. kb-nls-sign-convention dictates a flipped kinetic propagator exp(+i k^2 dt/2) for the user's +Q sign — the Psi propagator stays UNITARY (not parabolic-unstable) under sign flip, only the dispersion sign reverses. kb-nls-madelung-psi-structural-coupling is REJECTED because off-Mcs, u is independent (NOT u:=Im(conj(Psi)*Psi_x)); we must carry u as a separate field. kb-nls-split-linear-phase mandates phi=0.5x+phi_tilde split. kb-nls-mcs-not-attractor-standard-sign motivates the question itself and warns S6 results may not transfer; we re-test under the user's literal sign. BKdV MUSCL is rejected: no shock in u initially (smooth cos perturbation), spectral on u suffices for E1.

observation: Blew up at step 1, t=0.001. RuntimeWarning: invalid value in sqrt — N field went negative during the first RK4 sub-step. min(N0)=1.12e-25 (deep noise floor); Q-singularity diverges immediately when dispersive sub-step drives N below zero. Exactly the failure mode of kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure.

rationale: E1 confirmed direct (u, N, phi) RK4 is a dead-end under user's sign — same failure pattern as standard-sign experiments. E2 escalates ONE component: switch to Madelung-Psi representation for the (N, phi) sector with kinetic propagator exp(+i k^2 dt/2) (sign flipped from standard NLS to absorb user's +Q term). u remains a separate field. Strang-split Psi step (linear in Fourier with +sign, nonlinear in real space) + RK4 step on m = u - N*phi_x. This matches the bank's recommended escalation (kb-nls-madelung-psi-handles-zero-density, kb-nls-strang-splitstep-bright-soliton) adapted for the user's sign.

ic: same as E1 (kappa=1, A=1.5, sech^2 N0 at x=-5, phi0=0.5 x, u0 = 0.5 N0 + 0.1 cos(2 pi x/L)); single epsilon=0.1.
method: Spectral RK4 on (Psi_tilde, m) where Psi_tilde=sqrt(N) exp(i phi_tilde), phi=0.5 x + phi_tilde, m=u-N phi_x. Psi_t computed directly from N_t and phi_tilde_t (user's PDEs). 2/3 dealiasing on every nonlinear product. Soft regularization eps_reg=1e-10 for sqrt(N+eps_reg). dt=5e-4. N=|Psi_tilde|^2 is non-negative by construction so the sqrt-of-negative catastrophe of E1 cannot happen. m equation uses m_t=-u m_x - 2 u_x m derived from m_t+(u m)_x + m u_x=0.
bank_use_rationale: kb-nls-madelung-psi-handles-zero-density is the central asset: |Psi|^2 >= 0 STRUCTURALLY, which is what fixes E1. kb-nls-sign-convention warns that the Madelung-Psi 'Q-cancellation' trick (which makes standard NLS Madelung work cleanly) does NOT happen under the user's +sign — so I retain Q explicitly in the Psi-RHS and rely on soft regularization rather than Q-elimination. kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic + kb-nls-split-linear-phase + kb-nls-hard-floor-counterproductive give the auxiliary numerical choices. kb-nls-mass-conservation-not-sufficient and kb-nls-mcs-not-sufficient dictate monitoring multiple diagnostics. BKdV MUSCL and IMEX-CN entries are rejected: no shock, no KdV operator in this system.

observation: Blew up at step 67 (t=0.0335) with eps_reg=1e-4; at step 4 (t=0.002) with eps_reg=1e-10. Failure mode: phi_x reconstruction (=N_phi_tilde_x/safe_N) and Psi_t = Psi*(N_t/(2N) + i phi_t) both involve 1/N divisions that amplify noise into the soliton tails. min(N) is 1e-25 but truncation/aliasing artifacts in N_phi_tilde_x and N_t inject non-zero values into tails, and dividing them by safe_N (even safe_N=1e-4) gives O(1) values that propagate via dealias/multiply to overflow. The fundamental issue: under the user's +Q sign the Madelung-Psi correspondence is NOT trivializing (2Q remains explicit) so we cannot rely on |Psi|^2>=0 alone for stability; the off-Mcs phi-derivative reconstruction itself is fragile in tails.

rationale: E2 confirmed that even with Psi-state, naive RK4 of the user-sign B-NLS fails in tails because 1/N reconstruction blows up. E3 makes ONE more component change: Strang split-step with the LINEAR part (Psi_t = +(i/2) Psi_xx) handled EXACTLY in Fourier as exp(+i k^2 dt/2) (the user's-sign kinetic), and the NONLINEAR part as a pointwise (or local) RK4 sub-step. Key trick: in the nonlinear sub-step do NOT divide by N anywhere — instead use the conservative/'flux-form' update for (N, phi_tilde) where every nonlinear product is N-bounded.  Also: aggressive exponential filter on Psi to suppress aliasing in tails.

method: Strang split-step on (Psi_tilde, m). Linear sub-step Psi_k -> exp(+i k^2 dt/2) Psi_k (user's flipped kinetic), unitary in Fourier. Nonlinear sub-step pointwise: Psi -> Psi * exp(-alpha dt) * exp(-i beta dt) with alpha=(u N)_x/(2N) (continuity divergence) and beta=2 Q + u phi_x - 2 kappa N (user-sign potential), coefficients frozen at start (Strang accuracy O(dt^2)). RK4 on m with frozen-coefficient u. Exponential filter exp(-36 (k/k_max)^36) on Psi every step, on top of 2/3 dealiasing. dt=5e-4, Nx=256, T=12.
bank_use_rationale: kb-nls-strang-splitstep-bright-soliton is the central asset: Strang gives O(dt^2) accuracy + machine-precision mass conservation via the unitary linear sub-step. kb-nls-sign-convention requires the +i k^2 dt/2 kinetic propagator. kb-nls-madelung-psi-handles-zero-density supplies the structural |Psi|^2 >=0. kb-nls-energy-drift-vs-mass-drift and ...-not-sufficient mandate multiple diagnostics. kb-nls-lie-splitting-uneconomical and kb-nls-etd-rk1-mass-destruction motivate choosing Strang over alternatives. BKdV MUSCL rejected (no shock); BKdV IFRK4 rejected per kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup; kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts cited as cross-bank support for dealiasing.
